```text
1	# UIGen
2	
3	AI-powered React component generator with live preview.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Node.js 18+
8	- npm
9	
10	## Setup
11	
12	1. **Optional** Edit `.env` and replace `your-api-key-here` with your Anthropic API key from [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys):
13	
14	```
15	ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
16	```
17	
18	The project runs without an API key — it falls back to a mock provider that returns canned components instead of calling Claude. If you leave the placeholder unchanged, you'll get the mock.
19	
20	2. Install dependencies and initialize the database:
21	
22	```bash
23	npm run setup
24	```
25	
26	> **Don't run `npm audit fix`.** Dependencies are pinned to specific versions that work together, and `audit fix` can bump packages past compatible versions and break the app. Known security issues are addressed by updating the pinned versions directly — most recently, Next.js was bumped to a patched release to fix the React2Shell vulnerability (CVE-2025-55182 / CVE-2025-66478). If your scanner still flags something, raise it rather than running `audit fix`.
27	
28	This command will:
29	
30	- Install all dependencies
31	- Generate Prisma client
32	- Run database migrations
33	
34	## Running the Application
35	
36	### Development
37	
38	```bash
39	npm run dev
40	```
41	
42	Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000)
43	
44	## Usage
45	
46	1. Sign up or continue as anonymous user
47	2. Describe the React component you want to create in the chat
48	3. View generated components in real-time preview
49	4. Switch to Code view to see and edit the generated files
50	5. Continue iterating with the AI to refine your components
51	
52	## Features
53	
54	- AI-powered component generation using Claude
55	- Live preview with hot reload
56	- Virtual file system (no files written to disk)
57	- Syntax highlighting and code editor
58	- Component persistence for registered users
59	- Export generated code
60	
61	## Tech Stack
62	
63	- Next.js 15 with App Router
64	- React 19
65	- TypeScript
66	- Tailwind CSS v4
67	- Prisma with SQLite
68	- Anthropic Claude AI
69	- Vercel AI SDK
70
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	export interface FileNode {
2	  type: "file" | "directory";
3	  name: string;
4	  path: string;
5	  content?: string;
6	  children?: Map<string, FileNode>;
7	}
8	
9	export class VirtualFileSystem {
10	  private files: Map<string, FileNode> = new Map();
11	  private root: FileNode;
12	
13	  constructor() {
14	    this.root = {
15	      type: "directory",
16	      name: "/",
17	      path: "/",
18	      children: new Map(),
19	    };
20	    this.files.set("/", this.root);
21	  }
22	
23	  private normalizePath(path: string): string {
24	    // Ensure path starts with /
25	    if (!path.startsWith("/")) {
26	      path = "/" + path;
27	    }
28	    // Remove trailing slash except for root
29	    if (path !== "/" && path.endsWith("/")) {
30	      path = path.slice(0, -1);
31	    }
32	    // Normalize multiple slashes
33	    path = path.replace(/\/+/g, "/");
34	    return path;
35	  }
36	
37	  private getParentPath(path: string): string {
38	    const normalized = this.normalizePath(path);
39	    if (normalized === "/") return "/";
40	    const parts = normalized.split("/");
41	    parts.pop();
42	    return parts.length === 1 ? "/" : parts.join("/");
43	  }
44	
45	  private getFileName(path: string): string {
46	    const normalized = this.normalizePath(path);
47	    if (normalized === "/") return "/";
48	    const parts = normalized.split("/");
49	    return parts[parts.length - 1];
50	  }
51	
52	  private getParentNode(path: string): FileNode | null {
53	    const parentPath = this.getParentPath(path);
54	    return this.files.get(parentPath) || null;
55	  }
56	
57	  createFile(path: string, content: string = ""): FileNode | null {
58	    const normalized = this.normalizePath(path);
59	
60	    // Check if file already exists
61	    if (this.files.has(normalized)) {
62	      return null;
63	    }
64	
65	    // Create parent directories if they don't exist
66	    const parts = normalized.split("/").filter(Boolean);
67	    let currentPath = "";
68	
69	    for (let i = 0; i < parts.length - 1; i++) {
70	      currentPath += "/" + parts[i];
71	      if (!this.exists(currentPath)) {
72	        this.createDirectory(currentPath);
73	      }
74	    }
75	
76	    const parent = this.getParentNode(normalized);
77	    if (!parent || parent.type !== "directory") {
78	      return null;
79	    }
80	
81	    const fileName = this.getFileName(normalized);
82	    const file: FileNode = {
83	      type: "file",
84	      name: fileName,
85	      path: normalized,
86	      content,
87	    };
88	
89	    this.files.set(normalized, file);
90	    parent.children!.set(fileName, file);
91	
92	    return file;
93	  }
94	
95	  createDirectory(path: string): FileNode | null {
96	    const normalized = this.normalizePath(path);
97	
98	    // Check if directory already exists
99	    if (this.files.has(normalized)) {
100	      return null;
101	    }
102	
103	    const parent = this.getParentNode(normalized);
104	    if (!parent || parent.type !== "directory") {
105	      return null;
106	    }
107	
108	    const dirName = this.getFileName(normalized);
109	    const directory: FileNode = {
110	      type: "directory",
111	      name: dirName,
112	      path: normalized,
113	      children: new Map(),
114	    };
115	
116	    this.files.set(normalized, directory);
117	    parent.children!.set(dirName, directory);
118	
119	    return directory;
120	  }
121	
122	  readFile(path: string): string | null {
123	    const normalized = this.normalizePath(path);
124	    const file = this.files.get(normalized);
125	
126	    if (!file || file.type !== "file") {
127	      return null;
128	    }
129	
130	    return file.content || "";
131	  }
132	
133	  updateFile(path: string, content: string): boolean {
134	    const normalized = this.normalizePath(path);
135	    const file = this.files.get(normalized);
136	
137	    if (!file || file.type !== "file") {
138	      return false;
139	    }
140	
141	    file.content = content;
142	    return true;
143	  }
144	
145	  deleteFile(path: string): boolean {
146	    const normalized = this.normalizePath(path);
147	    const file = this.files.get(normalized);
148	
149	    if (!file || normalized === "/") {
150	      return false;
151	    }
152	
153	    const parent = this.getParentNode(normalized);
154	    if (!parent || parent.type !== "directory") {
155	      return false;
156	    }
157	
158	    // If it's a directory, remove all children recursively
159	    if (file.type === "directory" && file.children) {
160	      for (const [_, child] of file.children) {
161	        this.deleteFile(child.path);
162	      }
163	    }
164	
165	    parent.children!.delete(file.name);
166	    this.files.delete(normalized);
167	
168	    return true;
169	  }
170	
171	  rename(oldPath: string, newPath: string): boolean {
172	    const normalizedOld = this.normalizePath(oldPath);
173	    const normalizedNew = this.normalizePath(newPath);
174	
175	    // Can't rename root
176	    if (normalizedOld === "/" || normalizedNew === "/") {
177	      return false;
178	    }
179	
180	    // Check if source exists
181	    const sourceNode = this.files.get(normalizedOld);
182	    if (!sourceNode) {
183	      return false;
184	    }
185	
186	    // Check if destination already exists
187	    if (this.files.has(normalizedNew)) {
188	      return false;
189	    }
190	
191	    // Get parent of source
192	    const oldParent = this.getParentNode(normalizedOld);
193	    if (!oldParent || oldParent.type !== "directory") {
194	      return false;
195	    }
196	
197	    // Create parent directories for destination if needed
198	    const newParentPath = this.getParentPath(normalizedNew);
199	    if (!this.exists(newParentPath)) {
200	      const parts = newParentPath.split("/").filter(Boolean);
201	      let currentPath = "";
202	
203	      for (const part of parts) {
204	        currentPath += "/" + part;
205	        if (!this.exists(currentPath)) {
206	          this.createDirectory(currentPath);
207	        }
208	      }
209	    }
210	
211	    // Get parent of destination
212	    const newParent = this.getParentNode(normalizedNew);
213	    if (!newParent || newParent.type !== "directory") {
214	      return false;
215	    }
216	
217	    // Remove from old parent
218	    oldParent.children!.delete(sourceNode.name);
219	
220	    // Update the node's path and name
221	    const newName = this.getFileName(normalizedNew);
222	    sourceNode.name = newName;
223	    sourceNode.path = normalizedNew;
224	
225	    // Add to new parent
226	    newParent.children!.set(newName, sourceNode);
227	
228	    // Update in files map
229	    this.files.delete(normalizedOld);
230	    this.files.set(normalizedNew, sourceNode);
231	
232	    // If it's a directory, update all children paths recursively
233	    if (sourceNode.type === "directory" && sourceNode.children) {
234	      this.updateChildrenPaths(sourceNode);
235	    }
236	
237	    return true;
238	  }
239	
240	  private updateChildrenPaths(node: FileNode): void {
241	    if (node.type === "directory" && node.children) {
242	      for (const [_, child] of node.children) {
243	        const oldChildPath = child.path;
244	        child.path = node.path + "/" + child.name;
245	
246	        // Update in files map
247	        this.files.delete(oldChildPath);
248	        this.files.set(child.path, child);
249	
250	        // Recursively update children if it's a directory
251	        if (child.type === "directory") {
252	          this.updateChildrenPaths(child);
253	        }
254	      }
255	    }
256	  }
257	
258	  exists(path: string): boolean {
259	    const normalized = this.normalizePath(path);
260	    return this.files.has(normalized);
261	  }
262	
263	  getNode(path: string): FileNode | null {
264	    const normalized = this.normalizePath(path);
265	    return this.files.get(normalized) || null;
266	  }
267	
268	  listDirectory(path: string): FileNode[] | null {
269	    const normalized = this.normalizePath(path);
270	    const dir = this.files.get(normalized);
271	
272	    if (!dir || dir.type !== "directory") {
273	      return null;
274	    }
275	
276	    return Array.from(dir.children?.values() || []);
277	  }
278	
279	  getAllFiles(): Map<string, string> {
280	    const fileMap = new Map<string, string>();
281	
282	    for (const [path, node] of this.files) {
283	      if (node.type === "file") {
284	        fileMap.set(path, node.content || "");
285	      }
286	    }
287	
288	    return fileMap;
289	  }
290	
291	  serialize(): Record<string, FileNode> {
292	    const result: Record<string, FileNode> = {};
293	
294	    for (const [path, node] of this.files) {
295	      // Create a shallow copy without the Map children to avoid serialization issues
296	      if (node.type === "directory") {
297	        result[path] = {
298	          type: node.type,
299	          name: node.name,
300	          path: node.path,
301	        };
302	      } else {
303	        result[path] = {
304	          type: node.type,
305	          name: node.name,
306	          path: node.path,
307	          content: node.content,
308	        };
309	      }
310	    }
311	
312	    return result;
313	  }
314	
315	  deserialize(data: Record<string, string>): void {
316	    // Clear existing files except root
317	    this.files.clear();
318	    this.root.children?.clear();
319	    this.files.set("/", this.root);
320	
321	    // Sort paths to ensure parent directories are created first
322	    const paths = Object.keys(data).sort();
323	
324	    for (const path of paths) {
325	      const parts = path.split("/").filter(Boolean);
326	      let currentPath = "";
327	
328	      // Create parent directories if they don't exist
329	      for (let i = 0; i < parts.length - 1; i++) {
330	        currentPath += "/" + parts[i];
331	        if (!this.exists(currentPath)) {
332	          this.createDirectory(currentPath);
333	        }
334	      }
335	
336	      // Create the file
337	      this.createFile(path, data[path]);
338	    }
339	  }
340	
341	  deserializeFromNodes(data: Record<string, FileNode>): void {
342	    // Clear existing files except root
343	    this.files.clear();
344	    this.root.children?.clear();
345	    this.files.set("/", this.root);
346	
347	    // Sort paths to ensure parent directories are created first
348	    const paths = Object.keys(data).sort();
349	
350	    for (const path of paths) {
351	      if (path === "/") continue; // Skip root
352	
353	      const node = data[path];
354	      const parts = path.split("/").filter(Boolean);
355	      let currentPath = "";
356	
357	      // Create parent directories if they don't exist
358	      for (let i = 0; i < parts.length - 1; i++) {
359	        currentPath += "/" + parts[i];
360	        if (!this.exists(currentPath)) {
361	          this.createDirectory(currentPath);
362	        }
363	      }
364	
365	      // Create the file or directory
366	      if (node.type === "file") {
367	        this.createFile(path, node.content || "");
368	      } else if (node.type === "directory") {
369	        this.createDirectory(path);
370	      }
371	    }
372	  }
373	
374	  // Text editor command implementations
375	  viewFile(path: string, viewRange?: [number, number]): string {
376	    const file = this.getNode(path);
377	    if (!file) {
378	      return `File not found: ${path}`;
379	    }
380	
381	    // If it's a directory, list its contents
382	    if (file.type === "directory") {
383	      const children = this.listDirectory(path);
384	      if (!children || children.length === 0) {
385	        return "(empty directory)";
386	      }
387	
388	      return children
389	        .sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name))
390	        .map((child) => {
391	          const prefix = child.type === "directory" ? "[DIR]" : "[FILE]";
392	          return `${prefix} ${child.name}`;
393	        })
394	        .join("\n");
395	    }
396	
397	    // For files, show content
398	    const content = file.content || "";
399	
400	    // Handle view_range if provided
401	    if (viewRange && viewRange.length === 2) {
402	      const lines = content.split("\n");
403	      const [start, end] = viewRange;
404	      const startLine = Math.max(1, start);
405	      const endLine = end === -1 ? lines.length : Math.min(lines.length, end);
406	
407	      const viewedLines = lines.slice(startLine - 1, endLine);
408	      return viewedLines
409	        .map((line, index) => `${startLine + index}\t${line}`)
410	        .join("\n");
411	    }
412	
413	    // Return full file with line numbers
414	    const lines = content.split("\n");
415	    return (
416	      lines.map((line, index) => `${index + 1}\t${line}`).join("\n") ||
417	      "(empty file)"
418	    );
419	  }
420	
421	  createFileWithParents(path: string, content: string = ""): string {
422	    // Check if file already exists
423	    if (this.exists(path)) {
424	      return `Error: File already exists: ${path}`;
425	    }
426	
427	    // Create parent directories if they don't exist
428	    const parts = path.split("/").filter(Boolean);
429	    let currentPath = "";
430	
431	    for (let i = 0; i < parts.length - 1; i++) {
432	      currentPath += "/" + parts[i];
433	      if (!this.exists(currentPath)) {
434	        this.createDirectory(currentPath);
435	      }
436	    }
437	
438	    // Create the file
439	    this.createFile(path, content);
440	    return `File created: ${path}`;
441	  }
442	
443	  replaceInFile(path: string, oldStr: string, newStr: string): string {
444	    const file = this.getNode(path);
445	    if (!file) {
446	      return `Error: File not found: ${path}`;
447	    }
448	
449	    if (file.type !== "file") {
450	      return `Error: Cannot edit a directory: ${path}`;
451	    }
452	
453	    const content = this.readFile(path) || "";
454	
455	    // Check if old_str exists in the file
456	    if (!oldStr || !content.includes(oldStr)) {
457	      return `Error: String not found in file: "${oldStr}"`;
458	    }
459	
460	    // Count occurrences
461	    const occurrences = (
462	      content.match(
463	        new RegExp(oldStr.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&"), "g")
464	      ) || []
465	    ).length;
466	
467	    // Replace all occurrences
468	    const updatedContent = content.split(oldStr).join(newStr || "");
469	    this.updateFile(path, updatedContent);
470	
471	    return `Replaced ${occurrences} occurrence(s) of the string in ${path}`;
472	  }
473	
474	  insertInFile(path: string, insertLine: number, text: string): string {
475	    const file = this.getNode(path);
476	    if (!file) {
477	      return `Error: File not found: ${path}`;
478	    }
479	
480	    if (file.type !== "file") {
481	      return `Error: Cannot edit a directory: ${path}`;
482	    }
483	
484	    const content = this.readFile(path) || "";
485	    const lines = content.split("\n");
486	
487	    // Validate insert_line
488	    if (
489	      insertLine === undefined ||
490	      insertLine < 0 ||
491	      insertLine > lines.length
492	    ) {
493	      return `Error: Invalid line number: ${insertLine}. File has ${lines.length} lines.`;
494	    }
495	
496	    // Insert the text
497	    lines.splice(insertLine, 0, text || "");
498	    const updatedContent = lines.join("\n");
499	    this.updateFile(path, updatedContent);
500	
501	    return `Text inserted at line ${insertLine} in ${path}`;
502	  }
503	
504	  reset(): void {
505	    // Clear all files and reset to initial state
506	    this.files.clear();
507	    this.root = {
508	      type: "directory",
509	      name: "/",
510	      path: "/",
511	      children: new Map(),
512	    };
513	    this.files.set("/", this.root);
514	  }
515	}
516	
517	export const fileSystem = new VirtualFileSystem();
518

1	import type { FileNode } from "@/lib/file-system";
2	import { VirtualFileSystem } from "@/lib/file-system";
3	import { streamText, appendResponseMessages } from "ai";
4	import { buildStrReplaceTool } from "@/lib/tools/str-replace";
5	import { buildFileManagerTool } from "@/lib/tools/file-manager";
6	import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
7	import { getSession } from "@/lib/auth";
8	import { getLanguageModel } from "@/lib/provider";
9	import { generationPrompt } from "@/lib/prompts/generation";
10	
11	export async function POST(req: Request) {
12	  const {
13	    messages,
14	    files,
15	    projectId,
16	  }: { messages: any[]; files: Record<string, FileNode>; projectId?: string } =
17	    await req.json();
18	
19	  messages.unshift({
20	    role: "system",
21	    content: generationPrompt,
22	    providerOptions: {
23	      anthropic: { cacheControl: { type: "ephemeral" } },
24	    },
25	  });
26	
27	  // Reconstruct the VirtualFileSystem from serialized data
28	  const fileSystem = new VirtualFileSystem();
29	  fileSystem.deserializeFromNodes(files);
30	
31	  const model = getLanguageModel();
32	  // Use fewer steps for mock provider to prevent repetition
33	  const isMockProvider = !process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY;
34	  const result = streamText({
35	    model,
36	    messages,
37	    maxTokens: 10_000,
38	    maxSteps: isMockProvider ? 4 : 40,
39	    onError: (err: any) => {
40	      console.error(err);
41	    },
42	    tools: {
43	      str_replace_editor: buildStrReplaceTool(fileSystem),
44	      file_manager: buildFileManagerTool(fileSystem),
45	    },
46	    onFinish: async ({ response }) => {
47	      // Save to project if projectId is provided and user is authenticated
48	      if (projectId) {
49	        try {
50	          // Check if user is authenticated
51	          const session = await getSession();
52	          if (!session) {
53	            console.error("User not authenticated, cannot save project");
54	            return;
55	          }
56	
57	          // Get the messages from the response
58	          const responseMessages = response.messages || [];
59	          // Combine original messages with response messages
60	          const allMessages = appendResponseMessages({
61	            messages: [...messages.filter((m) => m.role !== "system")],
62	            responseMessages,
63	          });
64	
65	          await prisma.project.update({
66	            where: {
67	              id: projectId,
68	              userId: session.userId,
69	            },
70	            data: {
71	              messages: JSON.stringify(allMessages),
72	              data: JSON.stringify(fileSystem.serialize()),
73	            },
74	          });
75	        } catch (error) {
76	          console.error("Failed to save project data:", error);
77	        }
78	      }
79	    },
80	  });
81	
82	  return result.toDataStreamResponse();
83	}
84	
85	export const maxDuration = 120;

424	
425	  async doGenerate(
426	    options: Parameters<LanguageModelV1["doGenerate"]>[0]
427	  ): Promise<Awaited<ReturnType<LanguageModelV1["doGenerate"]>>> {
428	    const userPrompt = this.extractUserPrompt(options.prompt);
429	
430	    // Collect all stream parts
431	    const parts: LanguageModelV1StreamPart[] = [];
432	    for await (const part of this.generateMockStream(
433	      options.prompt,
434	      userPrompt
435	    )) {
436	      parts.push(part);
437	    }
438	
439	    // Build response from parts
440	    const textParts = parts
441	      .filter((p) => p.type === "text-delta")
442	      .map((p) => (p as any).textDelta)
443	      .join("");
444	
445	    const toolCalls = parts
446	      .filter((p) => p.type === "tool-call")
447	      .map((p) => ({
448	        toolCallType: "function" as const,
449	        toolCallId: (p as any).toolCallId,
450	        toolName: (p as any).toolName,
451	        args: (p as any).args,
452	      }));
453	
454	    // Get finish reason from finish part
455	    const finishPart = parts.find((p) => p.type === "finish") as any;
456	    const finishReason = finishPart?.finishReason || "stop";
457	
458	    return {
459	      text: textParts,
460	      toolCalls,
461	      finishReason: finishReason as any,
462	      usage: {
463	        promptTokens: 100,
464	        completionTokens: 200,
465	      },
466	      warnings: [],
467	      rawCall: {
468	        rawPrompt: options.prompt,
469	        rawSettings: {
470	          maxTokens: options.maxTokens,
471	          temperature: options.temperature,
472	        },
473	      },
474	    };
475	  }
476	
477	  async doStream(
478	    options: Parameters<LanguageModelV1["doStream"]>[0]
479	  ): Promise<Awaited<ReturnType<LanguageModelV1["doStream"]>>> {
480	    const userPrompt = this.extractUserPrompt(options.prompt);
481	    const self = this;
482	
483	    const stream = new ReadableStream<LanguageModelV1StreamPart>({
484	      async start(controller) {
485	        try {
486	          const generator = self.generateMockStream(options.prompt, userPrompt);
487	          for await (const chunk of generator) {
488	            controller.enqueue(chunk);
489	          }
490	          controller.close();
491	        } catch (error) {
492	          controller.error(error);
493	        }
494	      },
495	    });
496	
497	    return {
498	      stream,
499	      warnings: [],
500	      rawCall: {
501	        rawPrompt: options.prompt,
502	        rawSettings: {},
503	      },
504	      rawResponse: { headers: {} },
505	    };
506	  }
507	}
508	
509	export function getLanguageModel() {
510	  const apiKey = process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY?.trim();
511	
512	  if (!apiKey || apiKey === "your-api-key-here") {
513	    console.log(
514	      "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set (or is still the placeholder). " +
515	        "Using the mock provider — responses will be canned. " +
516	        "Set a real key in .env to generate components with Claude."
517	    );
518	    return new MockLanguageModel("mock-" + MODEL);
519	  }
520	
521	  return anthropic(MODEL);
522	}
523

1	import * as Babel from "@babel/standalone";
2	
3	export interface TransformResult {
4	  code: string;
5	  error?: string;
6	  missingImports?: Set<string>;
7	  cssImports?: Set<string>;
8	}
9	
10	// Helper to create a placeholder module
11	function createPlaceholderModule(componentName: string): string {
12	  return `
13	import React from 'react';
14	const ${componentName} = function() {
15	  return React.createElement('div', {}, null);
16	}
17	export default ${componentName};
18	export { ${componentName} };
19	`;
20	}
21	
22	
23	export function transformJSX(
24	  code: string,
25	  filename: string,
26	  existingFiles: Set<string>
27	): TransformResult {
28	  try {
29	    const isTypeScript = filename.endsWith(".ts") || filename.endsWith(".tsx");
30	
31	    // Pre-process imports to handle missing files
32	    let processedCode = code;
33	    const importRegex =
34	      /import\s+(?:{[^}]+}|[^,\s]+)?\s*(?:,\s*{[^}]+})?\s+from\s+['"]([^'"]+)['"]/g;
35	    const imports = new Set<string>();
36	    const cssImports = new Set<string>();
37	
38	    // Detect CSS imports
39	    const cssImportRegex = /import\s+['"]([^'"]+\.css)['"]/g;
40	    let cssMatch;
41	    while ((cssMatch = cssImportRegex.exec(code)) !== null) {
42	      cssImports.add(cssMatch[1]);
43	    }
44	
45	    // Remove CSS imports from code
46	    processedCode = processedCode.replace(cssImportRegex, '');
47	
48	    let match;
49	    while ((match = importRegex.exec(code)) !== null) {
50	      // Skip CSS files from regular imports
51	      if (!match[1].endsWith('.css')) {
52	        imports.add(match[1]);
53	      }
54	    }
55	
56	    const result = Babel.transform(processedCode, {
57	      filename,
58	      presets: [
59	        ["react", { runtime: "automatic" }],
60	        ...(isTypeScript ? ["typescript"] : []),
61	      ],
62	      plugins: [],
63	    });
64	
65	    return {
66	      code: result.code || "",
67	      missingImports: imports,
68	      cssImports: cssImports,
69	    };
70	  } catch (error) {
71	    return {
72	      code: "",
73	      error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown transform error",
74	    };
75	  }
76	}
77	
78	export function createBlobURL(
79	  code: string,
80	  mimeType: string = "application/javascript"
81	): string {
82	  const blob = new Blob([code], { type: mimeType });
83	  return URL.createObjectURL(blob);
84	}
85	
86	export interface ImportMapResult {
87	  importMap: string;
88	  styles: string;
89	  errors: Array<{ path: string; error: string }>;
90	}
91	
92	export function createImportMap(files: Map<string, string>): ImportMapResult {
93	  const imports: Record<string, string> = {
94	    react: "https://esm.sh/react@19",
95	    "react-dom": "https://esm.sh/react-dom@19",
96	    "react-dom/client": "https://esm.sh/react-dom@19/client",
97	    "react/jsx-runtime": "https://esm.sh/react@19/jsx-runtime",
98	    "react/jsx-dev-runtime": "https://esm.sh/react@19/jsx-dev-runtime",
99	  };
100	
101	  // Transform each file and create blob URLs
102	  const transformedFiles = new Map<string, string>();
103	  const existingFiles = new Set(files.keys());
104	  const allImports = new Set<string>();
105	  const allCssImports = new Set<{ from: string; cssPath: string }>();
106	  let collectedStyles = "";
107	  const errors: Array<{ path: string; error: string }> = [];
108	
109	  // First pass: transform all files and collect imports
110	  for (const [path, content] of files) {
111	    if (
112	      path.endsWith(".js") ||
113	      path.endsWith(".jsx") ||
114	      path.endsWith(".ts") ||
115	      path.endsWith(".tsx")
116	    ) {
117	      const { code, error, missingImports, cssImports } = transformJSX(
118	        content,
119	        path,
120	        existingFiles
121	      );
122	      
123	      if (error) {
124	        // Track error for this file
125	        errors.push({ path, error });
126	        // Skip processing this file entirely
127	        continue;
128	      }
129	      
130	      // Normal successful transform
131	      const blobUrl = createBlobURL(code);
132	      transformedFiles.set(path, blobUrl);
133	
134	      // Collect all imports
135	      if (missingImports) {
136	        missingImports.forEach((imp) => {
137	          // Check if this is a third-party package
138	          const isPackage = !imp.startsWith(".") && 
139	                            !imp.startsWith("/") && 
140	                            !imp.startsWith("@/");
141	          
142	          if (isPackage) {
143	            // Add third-party packages directly to import map
144	            imports[imp] = `https://esm.sh/${imp}`;
145	          } else {
146	            // Add local imports to be processed later
147	            allImports.add(imp);
148	          }
149	        });
150	      }
151	
152	      // Collect CSS imports
153	      if (cssImports) {
154	        cssImports.forEach((cssImport) => {
155	          allCssImports.add({ from: path, cssPath: cssImport });
156	        });
157	      }
158	
159	      // Add to import map with absolute path
160	      imports[path] = blobUrl;
161	
162	      // Also add without leading slash
163	      if (path.startsWith("/")) {
164	        imports[path.substring(1)] = blobUrl;
165	      }
166	
167	      // Add @/ alias support - maps @/ to root directory
168	      if (path.startsWith("/")) {
169	        imports["@" + path] = blobUrl;
170	        imports["@/" + path.substring(1)] = blobUrl;
171	      }
172	
173	      // Add entries without file extensions for all variations
174	      const pathWithoutExt = path.replace(/\.(jsx?|tsx?)$/, "");
175	      imports[pathWithoutExt] = blobUrl;
176	
177	      if (path.startsWith("/")) {
178	        imports[pathWithoutExt.substring(1)] = blobUrl;
179	        imports["@" + pathWithoutExt] = blobUrl;
180	        imports["@/" + pathWithoutExt.substring(1)] = blobUrl;
181	      }
182	    } else if (path.endsWith(".css")) {
183	      // Collect CSS file content
184	      collectedStyles += `/* ${path} */\n${content}\n\n`;
185	    }
186	  }
187	
188	  // Process CSS imports
189	  for (const { from, cssPath } of allCssImports) {
190	    // Resolve CSS path relative to the importing file
191	    let resolvedPath = cssPath;
192	    
193	    if (cssPath.startsWith("@/")) {
194	      // @/ alias points to root
195	      resolvedPath = cssPath.replace("@/", "/");
196	    } else if (cssPath.startsWith("./") || cssPath.startsWith("../")) {
197	      // Relative path
198	      const fromDir = from.substring(0, from.lastIndexOf("/"));
199	      resolvedPath = resolveRelativePath(fromDir, cssPath);
200	    }
201	
202	    // Check if CSS file exists
203	    if (files.has(resolvedPath)) {
204	      // Already processed in the loop above
205	    } else {
206	      // CSS file not found
207	      collectedStyles += `/* ${cssPath} not found */\n`;
208	    }
209	  }
210	
211	  // Second pass: create placeholder modules for missing imports
212	  for (const importPath of allImports) {
213	    // Skip if it's a known module or already exists
214	    if (imports[importPath] || importPath.startsWith("react")) {
215	      continue;
216	    }
217	
218	    // Check if this is a third-party package (no relative path indicators)
219	    const isPackage = !importPath.startsWith(".") && 
220	                      !importPath.startsWith("/") && 
221	                      !importPath.startsWith("@/");
222	
223	    if (isPackage) {
224	      // Handle third-party packages from esm.sh
225	      const packageUrl = `https://esm.sh/${importPath}`;
226	      imports[importPath] = packageUrl;
227	      continue;
228	    }
229	
230	    // Check if the import exists in any form (local files)
231	    let found = false;
232	    const variations = [
233	      importPath,
234	      importPath + ".jsx",
235	      importPath + ".tsx",
236	      importPath + ".js",
237	      importPath + ".ts",
238	      importPath.replace("@/", "/"),
239	      importPath.replace("@/", "/") + ".jsx",
240	      importPath.replace("@/", "/") + ".tsx",
241	    ];
242	
243	    for (const variant of variations) {
244	      if (imports[variant] || files.has(variant)) {
245	        found = true;
246	        break;
247	      }
248	    }
249	
250	    if (!found) {
251	      // Extract component name from path
252	      const match = importPath.match(/\/([^\/]+)$/);
253	      const componentName = match
254	        ? match[1]
255	        : importPath.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/g, "");
256	
257	      // Create placeholder module
258	      const placeholderCode = createPlaceholderModule(componentName);
259	      const placeholderUrl = createBlobURL(placeholderCode);
260	
261	      // Add all possible import variations
262	      imports[importPath] = placeholderUrl;
263	      if (importPath.startsWith("@/")) {
264	        imports[importPath.replace("@/", "/")] = placeholderUrl;
265	        imports[importPath.replace("@/", "")] = placeholderUrl;
266	      }
267	    }
268	  }
269	
270	  return {
271	    importMap: JSON.stringify({ imports }, null, 2),
272	    styles: collectedStyles,
273	    errors
274	  };
275	}
276	
277	// Helper function to resolve relative paths
278	function resolveRelativePath(fromDir: string, relativePath: string): string {
279	  const parts = fromDir.split("/").filter(Boolean);
280	  const relParts = relativePath.split("/");
281	  
282	  for (const part of relParts) {
283	    if (part === "..") {
284	      parts.pop();
285	    } else if (part !== ".") {
286	      parts.push(part);
287	    }
288	  }
289	  
290	  return "/" + parts.join("/");
291	}

442	
443	        return this.props.children;
444	      }
445	    }
446	
447	    async function loadApp() {
448	      try {
449	        const module = await import('${entryPointUrl}');
450	        const App = module.default || module.App;
451	        
452	        if (!App) {
453	          throw new Error('No default export or App export found in ${entryPoint}');
454	        }
455	
456	        const root = ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root'));
457	        root.render(
458	          React.createElement(ErrorBoundary, null,
459	            React.createElement(App)
460	          )
461	        );
462	      } catch (error) {
463	        console.error('Failed to load app:', error);
464	        console.error('Import map:', ${JSON.stringify(importMap)});
465	        document.getElementById('root').innerHTML = '<div class="error-boundary"><h2>Failed to load app</h2><pre>' + error.toString() + '</pre></div>';
466	      }
467	    }
468	
469	    loadApp();
470	  </script>` : ''}
471	</body>
472	</html>`;
473	}
474

1	"use client";
2	
3	import React, {
4	  createContext,
5	  useContext,
6	  useState,
7	  useCallback,
8	  useEffect,
9	} from "react";
10	import { VirtualFileSystem, FileNode } from "@/lib/file-system";
11	
12	interface ToolCall {
13	  toolName: string;
14	  args: any;
15	}
16	
17	interface FileSystemContextType {
18	  fileSystem: VirtualFileSystem;
19	  selectedFile: string | null;
20	  setSelectedFile: (path: string | null) => void;
21	  createFile: (path: string, content?: string) => void;
22	  updateFile: (path: string, content: string) => void;
23	  deleteFile: (path: string) => void;
24	  renameFile: (oldPath: string, newPath: string) => boolean;
25	  getFileContent: (path: string) => string | null;
26	  getAllFiles: () => Map<string, string>;
27	  refreshTrigger: number;
28	  handleToolCall: (toolCall: ToolCall) => void;
29	  reset: () => void;
30	}
31	
32	const FileSystemContext = createContext<FileSystemContextType | undefined>(
33	  undefined
34	);
35	
36	export function FileSystemProvider({
37	  children,
38	  fileSystem: providedFileSystem,
39	  initialData,
40	}: {
41	  children: React.ReactNode;
42	  fileSystem?: VirtualFileSystem;
43	  initialData?: Record<string, any>;
44	}) {
45	  const [fileSystem] = useState(() => {
46	    const fs = providedFileSystem || new VirtualFileSystem();
47	    if (initialData) {
48	      fs.deserializeFromNodes(initialData);
49	    }
50	    return fs;
51	  });
52	  const [selectedFile, setSelectedFile] = useState<string | null>(null);
53	  const [refreshTrigger, setRefreshTrigger] = useState(0);
54	
55	  const triggerRefresh = useCallback(() => {
56	    setRefreshTrigger((prev) => prev + 1);
57	  }, []);
58	
59	  useEffect(() => {
60	    if (!selectedFile) {
61	      const files = fileSystem.getAllFiles();
62	
63	      // Check if App.jsx exists
64	      if (files.has("/App.jsx")) {
65	        setSelectedFile("/App.jsx");
66	      } else {
67	        // Find first file in root directory
68	        const rootFiles = Array.from(files.keys())
69	          .filter((path) => {
70	            const parts = path.split("/").filter(Boolean);
71	            return parts.length === 1; // Root level file
72	          })
73	          .sort();
74	
75	        if (rootFiles.length > 0) {
76	          setSelectedFile(rootFiles[0]);
77	        }
78	      }
79	    }
80	  }, [selectedFile, fileSystem, refreshTrigger]);
81	
82	  const createFile = useCallback(
83	    (path: string, content: string = "") => {
84	      fileSystem.createFile(path, content);
85	      triggerRefresh();
86	    },
87	    [fileSystem, triggerRefresh]
88	  );
89	
90	  const updateFile = useCallback(
91	    (path: string, content: string) => {
92	      fileSystem.updateFile(path, content);
93	      triggerRefresh();
94	    },
95	    [fileSystem, triggerRefresh]
96	  );
97	
98	  const deleteFile = useCallback(
99	    (path: string) => {
100	      fileSystem.deleteFile(path);
101	      if (selectedFile === path) {
102	        setSelectedFile(null);
103	      }
104	      triggerRefresh();
105	    },
106	    [fileSystem, selectedFile, triggerRefresh]
107	  );
108	
109	  const renameFile = useCallback(
110	    (oldPath: string, newPath: string): boolean => {
111	      const success = fileSystem.rename(oldPath, newPath);
112	      if (success) {
113	        // Update selected file if it was renamed
114	        if (selectedFile === oldPath) {
115	          setSelectedFile(newPath);
116	        } else if (selectedFile && selectedFile.startsWith(oldPath + "/")) {
117	          // Update selected file if it's inside a renamed directory
118	          const relativePath = selectedFile.substring(oldPath.length);
119	          setSelectedFile(newPath + relativePath);
120	        }
121	        triggerRefresh();
122	      }
123	      return success;
124	    },
125	    [fileSystem, selectedFile, triggerRefresh]
126	  );
127	
128	  const getFileContent = useCallback(
129	    (path: string) => {
130	      return fileSystem.readFile(path);
131	    },
132	    [fileSystem]
133	  );
134	
135	  const getAllFiles = useCallback(() => {
136	    return fileSystem.getAllFiles();
137	  }, [fileSystem]);
138	
139	  const reset = useCallback(() => {
140	    fileSystem.reset();
141	    setSelectedFile(null);
142	    triggerRefresh();
143	  }, [fileSystem, triggerRefresh]);
144	
145	  const handleToolCall = useCallback(
146	    (toolCall: ToolCall) => {
147	      const { toolName, args } = toolCall;
148	
149	      // Handle str_replace_editor tool
150	      if (toolName === "str_replace_editor" && args) {
151	        const { command, path, file_text, old_str, new_str, insert_line } = args;
152	
153	        switch (command) {
154	          case "create":
155	            if (path && file_text !== undefined) {
156	              const result = fileSystem.createFileWithParents(path, file_text);
157	              if (!result.startsWith("Error:")) {
158	                createFile(path, file_text);
159	              }
160	            }
161	            break;
162	
163	          case "str_replace":
164	            if (path && old_str !== undefined && new_str !== undefined) {
165	              const result = fileSystem.replaceInFile(path, old_str, new_str);
166	              if (!result.startsWith("Error:")) {
167	                const content = fileSystem.readFile(path);
168	                if (content !== null) {
169	                  updateFile(path, content);
170	                }
171	              }
172	            }
173	            break;
174	
175	          case "insert":
176	            if (path && new_str !== undefined && insert_line !== undefined) {
177	              const result = fileSystem.insertInFile(path, insert_line, new_str);
178	              if (!result.startsWith("Error:")) {
179	                const content = fileSystem.readFile(path);
180	                if (content !== null) {
181	                  updateFile(path, content);
182	                }
183	              }
184	            }
185	            break;
186	        }
187	      }
188	
189	      // Handle file_manager tool
190	      if (toolName === "file_manager" && args) {
191	        const { command, path, new_path } = args;
192	
193	        switch (command) {
194	          case "rename":
195	            if (path && new_path) {
196	              renameFile(path, new_path);
197	            }
198	            break;
199	
200	          case "delete":
201	            if (path) {
202	              const success = fileSystem.deleteFile(path);
203	              if (success) {
204	                deleteFile(path);
205	              }
206	            }
207	            break;
208	        }
209	      }
210	    },
211	    [fileSystem, createFile, updateFile, deleteFile, renameFile]
212	  );
213	
214	  return (
215	    <FileSystemContext.Provider
216	      value={{
217	        fileSystem,
218	        selectedFile,
219	        setSelectedFile,
220	        createFile,
221	        updateFile,
222	        deleteFile,
223	        renameFile,
224	        getFileContent,
225	        getAllFiles,
226	        refreshTrigger,
227	        handleToolCall,
228	        reset,
229	      }}
230	    >
231	      {children}
232	    </FileSystemContext.Provider>
233	  );
234	}
235	
236	export function useFileSystem() {
237	  const context = useContext(FileSystemContext);
238	  if (!context) {
239	    throw new Error("useFileSystem must be used within a FileSystemProvider");
240	  }
241	  return context;
242	}
243
```
</details></tool-use>

1	import { z } from "zod";
2	import { VirtualFileSystem } from "@/lib/file-system";
3	
4	const TextEditorParameters = z.object({
5	  command: z.enum(["view", "create", "str_replace", "insert", "undo_edit"]),
6	  path: z.string(),
7	  file_text: z.string().optional(),
8	  insert_line: z.number().optional(),
9	  new_str: z.string().optional(),
10	  old_str: z.string().optional(),
11	  view_range: z.array(z.number()).optional(),
12	});
13	
14	export const buildStrReplaceTool = (fileSystem: VirtualFileSystem) => {
15	  return {
16	    id: "str_replace_editor" as const,
17	    args: {},
18	    parameters: TextEditorParameters,
19	    execute: async ({
20	      command,
21	      path,
22	      file_text,
23	      insert_line,
24	      new_str,
25	      old_str,
26	      view_range,
27	    }: z.infer<typeof TextEditorParameters>) => {
28	      switch (command) {
29	        case "view":
30	          return fileSystem.viewFile(
31	            path,
32	            view_range as [number, number] | undefined
33	          );
34	
35	        case "create":
36	          return fileSystem.createFileWithParents(path, file_text || "");
37	
38	        case "str_replace":
39	          return fileSystem.replaceInFile(path, old_str || "", new_str || "");
40	
41	        case "insert":
42	          return fileSystem.insertInFile(path, insert_line || 0, new_str || "");
43	
44	        case "undo_edit":
45	          return `Error: undo_edit command is not supported in this version. Use str_replace to revert changes.`;
46	      }
47	    },
48	  };
49	};
50

1	import { tool } from "ai";
2	import { z } from "zod";
3	import { VirtualFileSystem } from "../file-system";
4	
5	export function buildFileManagerTool(fileSystem: VirtualFileSystem) {
6	  return tool({
7	    description:
8	      'Rename or delete files or folders in the file system. Rename can be used to "move" a file. Rename will recursively create folders as required.',
9	    parameters: z.object({
10	      command: z
11	        .enum(["rename", "delete"])
12	        .describe("The operation to perform"),
13	      path: z
14	        .string()
15	        .describe("The path to the file or directory to rename or delete"),
16	      new_path: z
17	        .string()
18	        .optional()
19	        .describe("The new path. Only provide when renaming or moving a file."),
20	    }),
21	    execute: async ({ command, path, new_path }) => {
22	      if (command === "rename") {
23	        if (!new_path) {
24	          return {
25	            success: false,
26	            error: "new_path is required for rename command",
27	          };
28	        }
29	        const success = fileSystem.rename(path, new_path);
30	        if (success) {
31	          return {
32	            success: true,
33	            message: `Successfully renamed ${path} to ${new_path}`,
34	          };
35	        } else {
36	          return {
37	            success: false,
38	            error: `Failed to rename ${path} to ${new_path}`,
39	          };
40	        }
41	      } else if (command === "delete") {
42	        const success = fileSystem.deleteFile(path);
43	        if (success) {
44	          return { success: true, message: `Successfully deleted ${path}` };
45	        } else {
46	          return { success: false, error: `Failed to delete ${path}` };
47	        }
48	      }
49	
50	      return { success: false, error: "Invalid command" };
51	    },
52	  });
53	}
54

1	import "server-only";
2	import { SignJWT, jwtVerify } from "jose";
3	import { cookies } from "next/headers";
4	import { NextRequest } from "next/server";
5	
6	const JWT_SECRET = new TextEncoder().encode(
7	  process.env.JWT_SECRET || "development-secret-key"
8	);
9	
10	const COOKIE_NAME = "auth-token";
11	
12	export interface SessionPayload {
13	  userId: string;
14	  email: string;
15	  expiresAt: Date;
16	}
17	
18	export async function createSession(userId: string, email: string) {
19	  const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000); // 7 days
20	  const session: SessionPayload = { userId, email, expiresAt };
21	
22	  const token = await new SignJWT({ ...session })
23	    .setProtectedHeader({ alg: "HS256" })
24	    .setExpirationTime("7d")
25	    .setIssuedAt()
26	    .sign(JWT_SECRET);
27	
28	  const cookieStore = await cookies();
29	  cookieStore.set(COOKIE_NAME, token, {
30	    httpOnly: true,
31	    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production",
32	    sameSite: "lax",
33	    expires: expiresAt,
34	    path: "/",
35	  });
36	}
37	
38	export async function getSession(): Promise<SessionPayload | null> {
39	  const cookieStore = await cookies();
40	  const token = cookieStore.get(COOKIE_NAME)?.value;
41	
42	  if (!token) {
43	    return null;
44	  }
45	
46	  try {
47	    const { payload } = await jwtVerify(token, JWT_SECRET);
48	    return payload as unknown as SessionPayload;
49	  } catch (error) {
50	    return null;
51	  }
52	}
53	
54	export async function deleteSession() {
55	  const cookieStore = await cookies();
56	  cookieStore.delete(COOKIE_NAME);
57	}
58	
59	export async function verifySession(
60	  request: NextRequest
61	): Promise<SessionPayload | null> {
62	  const token = request.cookies.get(COOKIE_NAME)?.value;
63	
64	  if (!token) {
65	    return null;
66	  }
67	
68	  try {
69	    const { payload } = await jwtVerify(token, JWT_SECRET);
70	    return payload as unknown as SessionPayload;
71	  } catch (error) {
72	    return null;
73	  }
74	}
75

1	// Simple utility to track if anonymous user has created work
2	const STORAGE_KEY = "uigen_has_anon_work";
3	const DATA_KEY = "uigen_anon_data";
4	
5	export function setHasAnonWork(messages: any[], fileSystemData: any) {
6	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return;
7	  
8	  // Only set if there's actual content
9	  if (messages.length > 0 || Object.keys(fileSystemData).length > 1) { // > 1 because root "/" always exists
10	    sessionStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, "true");
11	    sessionStorage.setItem(DATA_KEY, JSON.stringify({ messages, fileSystemData }));
12	  }
13	}
14	
15	export function getHasAnonWork(): boolean {
16	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return false;
17	  return sessionStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY) === "true";
18	}
19	
20	export function getAnonWorkData(): { messages: any[], fileSystemData: any } | null {
21	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return null;
22	  
23	  const data = sessionStorage.getItem(DATA_KEY);
24	  if (!data) return null;
25	  
26	  try {
27	    return JSON.parse(data);
28	  } catch {
29	    return null;
30	  }
31	}
32	
33	export function clearAnonWork() {
34	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return;
35	  sessionStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY);
36	  sessionStorage.removeItem(DATA_KEY);
37	}
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
2	import type { NextRequest } from "next/server";
3	import { verifySession } from "@/lib/auth";
4	
5	export async function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
6	  const session = await verifySession(request);
7	
8	  // Protected routes that require authentication
9	  const protectedPaths = ["/api/projects", "/api/filesystem"];
10	  const isProtectedPath = protectedPaths.some((path) =>
11	    request.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith(path)
12	  );
13	
14	  if (isProtectedPath && !session) {
15	    return NextResponse.json(
16	      { error: "Authentication required" },
17	      { status: 401 }
18	    );
19	  }
20	
21	  return NextResponse.next();
22	}
23	
24	export const config = {
25	  matcher: [
26	    /*
27	     * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:
28	     * - _next/static (static files)
29	     * - _next/image (image optimization files)
30	     * - favicon.ico (favicon file)
31	     * - public folder
32	     */
33	    "/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\\.(?:svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp)$).*)",
34	  ],
35	};
```
</details></tool-use>

1	"use client";
2	
3	import {
4	  createContext,
5	  useContext,
6	  ReactNode,
7	  useEffect,
8	} from "react";
9	import { useChat as useAIChat } from "@ai-sdk/react";
10	import { Message } from "ai";
11	import { useFileSystem } from "./file-system-context";
12	import { setHasAnonWork } from "@/lib/anon-work-tracker";
13	
14	interface ChatContextProps {
15	  projectId?: string;
16	  initialMessages?: Message[];
17	}
18	
19	interface ChatContextType {
20	  messages: Message[];
21	  input: string;
22	  handleInputChange: (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLTextAreaElement>) => void;
23	  handleSubmit: (e: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) => void;
24	  status: string;
25	}
26	
27	const ChatContext = createContext<ChatContextType | undefined>(undefined);
28	
29	export function ChatProvider({
30	  children,
31	  projectId,
32	  initialMessages = [],
33	}: ChatContextProps & { children: ReactNode }) {
34	  const { fileSystem, handleToolCall } = useFileSystem();
35	
36	  const {
37	    messages,
38	    input,
39	    handleInputChange,
40	    handleSubmit,
41	    status,
42	  } = useAIChat({
43	    api: "/api/chat",
44	    initialMessages,
45	    body: {
46	      files: fileSystem.serialize(),
47	      projectId,
48	    },
49	    onToolCall: ({ toolCall }) => {
50	      handleToolCall(toolCall);
51	    },
52	  });
53	
54	  // Track anonymous work
55	  useEffect(() => {
56	    if (!projectId && messages.length > 0) {
57	      setHasAnonWork(messages, fileSystem.serialize());
58	    }
59	  }, [messages, fileSystem, projectId]);
60	
61	  return (
62	    <ChatContext.Provider
63	      value={{
64	        messages,
65	        input,
66	        handleInputChange,
67	        handleSubmit,
68	        status,
69	      }}
70	    >
71	      {children}
72	    </ChatContext.Provider>
73	  );
74	}
75	
76	export function useChat() {
77	  const context = useContext(ChatContext);
78	  if (context === undefined) {
79	    throw new Error("useChat must be used within a ChatProvider");
80	  }
81	  return context;
82	}
```
</details></tool-use>

1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useState } from "react";
4	import { useRouter } from "next/navigation";
5	import { signIn as signInAction, signUp as signUpAction } from "@/actions";
6	import { getAnonWorkData, clearAnonWork } from "@/lib/anon-work-tracker";
7	import { getProjects } from "@/actions/get-projects";
8	import { createProject } from "@/actions/create-project";
9	
10	export function useAuth() {
11	  const router = useRouter();
12	  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);
13	
14	  const handlePostSignIn = async () => {
15	    // Get any anonymous work
16	    const anonWork = getAnonWorkData();
17	
18	    if (anonWork && anonWork.messages.length > 0) {
19	      // Create a project with the anonymous work
20	      const project = await createProject({
21	        name: `Design from ${new Date().toLocaleTimeString()}`,
22	        messages: anonWork.messages,
23	        data: anonWork.fileSystemData,
24	      });
25	
26	      clearAnonWork();
27	      router.push(`/${project.id}`);
28	      return;
29	    }
30	
31	    // Otherwise, find the user's most recent project
32	    const projects = await getProjects();
33	
34	    if (projects.length > 0) {
35	      router.push(`/${projects[0].id}`);
36	      return;
37	    }
38	
39	    // If no projects exist, create a new one
40	    const newProject = await createProject({
41	      name: `New Design #${~~(Math.random() * 100000)}`,
42	      messages: [],
43	      data: {},
44	    });
45	
46	    router.push(`/${newProject.id}`);
47	  };
48	
49	  const signIn = async (email: string, password: string) => {
50	    setIsLoading(true);
51	    try {
52	      const result = await signInAction(email, password);
53	
54	      if (result.success) {
55	        await handlePostSignIn();
56	      }
57	
58	      return result;
59	    } finally {
60	      setIsLoading(false);
61	    }
62	  };
63	
64	  const signUp = async (email: string, password: string) => {
65	    setIsLoading(true);
66	    try {
67	      const result = await signUpAction(email, password);
68	
69	      if (result.success) {
70	        await handlePostSignIn();
71	      }
72	
73	      return result;
74	    } finally {
75	      setIsLoading(false);
76	    }
77	  };
78	
79	  return {
80	    signIn,
81	    signUp,
82	    isLoading,
83	  };
84	}
85
```

```md
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Overview

UIGen is an AI-powered React component generator with live preview. Users describe a component in a chat; Claude generates the code into a **virtual (in-memory) file system**, which is transpiled in the browser and rendered in a live preview iframe. No generated files are ever written to disk.

## Commands

\```bash
npm run setup        # install deps, generate Prisma client, run migrations (run this first)
npm run dev          # start dev server with Turbopack on :3000
npm run dev:daemon   # same, but backgrounded with output redirected to logs.txt
npm run build        # production build
npm run lint         # next lint
npm test             # run Vitest test suite
npm run db:reset     # force-reset the SQLite database (destructive)
\```

Run a single test file or pattern:

\```bash
npx vitest run src/lib/__tests__/file-system.test.ts
npx vitest run -t "createFile"     # filter by test name
\```

Do **not** run `npm audit fix` — dependencies are pinned to known-compatible versions and `audit fix` can break the app. Bump pinned versions directly instead (see README).

## Environment

- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in `.env` is **optional**. Without it (or if left as the `your-api-key-here` placeholder), the app falls back to `MockLanguageModel` (`src/lib/provider.ts`), which streams canned components instead of calling Claude. `getLanguageModel()` decides this at runtime.
- The model used when a key is present is `claude-haiku-4-5` (the `MODEL` constant in `src/lib/provider.ts`).
- `JWT_SECRET` signs auth cookies; defaults to a dev value if unset.

## Architecture

### Virtual file system (the core abstraction)
`src/lib/file-system.ts` defines `VirtualFileSystem`, an in-memory tree of `FileNode`s. This is the single source of truth for generated code and is used on **both** the server and the client:

- **Server** (`src/app/api/chat/route.ts`): reconstructs a fresh `VirtualFileSystem` from the serialized files in the request, exposes it to the AI tools, then `serialize()`s it back into the DB after generation.
- **Client** (`src/lib/contexts/file-system-context.tsx`): holds the live `VirtualFileSystem` the UI reads from. As tool calls stream in, `handleToolCall` mutates the FS and bumps a `refreshTrigger` to re-render the tree/editor/preview.

Serialization formats matter: `serialize()`/`deserializeFromNodes()` round-trip `FileNode` objects; `getAllFiles()` returns a flat `Map<path, content>` used by the transformer.

### AI tools
The model is given two tools (defined under `src/lib/tools/`), both built as closures over a `VirtualFileSystem` instance:
- `str_replace_editor` (`str-replace.ts`) — `view`/`create`/`str_replace`/`insert` text-editor commands.
- `file_manager` (`file-manager.ts`) — `rename`/`delete` files and directories.

The same tool-call args are interpreted **twice**: server-side they execute against the server FS (via `execute`); client-side `handleToolCall` in `file-system-context.tsx` replays them against the client FS. Keep these two interpretations in sync when changing tool behavior.

The system prompt that governs generation lives in `src/lib/prompts/generation.tsx`. Key rules it enforces: every project must have a root `/App.jsx` default export, styling is Tailwind-only, and non-library imports use the `@/` alias mapped to the FS root.

### Live preview / in-browser transpilation
`src/lib/transform/jsx-transformer.ts` makes the virtual FS runnable in an iframe **without a bundler**:
1. Each `.jsx/.tsx/.js/.ts` file is transpiled with `@babel/standalone` and turned into a blob URL.
2. An [import map](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/script/type/importmap) wires every local path (and its `@/`, extensionless, and leading-slash variants) to its blob URL; bare package imports resolve to `https://esm.sh/<pkg>`; React comes from esm.sh.
3. Missing local imports get a stubbed placeholder module so the preview still renders.
4. `createPreviewHTML` assembles the final HTML (Tailwind via CDN, an error boundary, and a syntax-error panel). Rendered by `src/components/preview/PreviewFrame.tsx`.

### Persistence & auth
- Prisma + SQLite. Schema in `prisma/schema.prisma`; the client is generated to **`src/generated/prisma`** (not the default location). Two models: `User` and `Project`. A `Project` stores `messages` and `data` (the serialized FS) as JSON strings; `userId` is nullable.
- Auth is JWT-in-an-httpOnly-cookie via `jose` (`src/lib/auth.ts`). `middleware.ts` guards `/api/projects` and `/api/filesystem`. Passwords hashed with bcrypt.
- Server mutations go through server actions in `src/actions/` (`create-project`, `get-projects`, `get-project`, plus auth actions re-exported from `src/actions/index.ts`).

### Anonymous → authenticated flow
Anonymous users can generate components without an account. Work is stashed in `sessionStorage` via `src/lib/anon-work-tracker.ts`. On sign-in/up, `use-auth.ts#handlePostSignIn` pulls that stashed work, creates a real `Project` from it, clears the stash, and routes to it. With no anon work, it opens the most recent project or creates a fresh one.

### UI composition
`src/app/page.tsx` (server) handles the auth redirect; `main-content.tsx` (client) is the root layout: a resizable two-panel UI with `FileSystemProvider` wrapping `ChatProvider`. Left panel = chat; right panel = Preview/Code tabs (FileTree + Monaco `CodeEditor`). The chat itself uses the Vercel AI SDK's `useChat` (`chat-context.tsx`), POSTing to `/api/chat`.

## Conventions

- Path alias `@/` → `src/` (configured in `tsconfig.json`, resolved in tests via `vite-tsconfig-paths`).
- Tests are colocated in `__tests__/` directories next to the code they cover; Vitest runs in a `jsdom` environment with React Testing Library.
- `shadcn/ui`-style primitives live in `src/components/ui/`; Tailwind v4.

```

I've created `CLAUDE.md`. Here's what it captures:

**Commands** — setup/dev/build/lint/test, plus how to run a single test (`npx vitest run <file>` / `-t <name>`), and the README's warning against `npm audit fix`.

**Environment** — the optional `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and the mock-provider fallback (the most surprising "gotcha" for a new instance — the app runs and generates without any key).

**Architecture** — the big-picture pieces that require reading several files to grasp:

- **Virtual file system** as the single source of truth, used on *both* server and client with specific serialization formats.
- **AI tools** (`str_replace_editor`, `file_manager`) and the key subtlety that tool-call args are interpreted twice — server-side `execute` and client-side `handleToolCall` — which must stay in sync.
- **In-browser transpilation** via Babel standalone + import maps + esm.sh, with no bundler, and the placeholder-module trick for missing imports.
- **Persistence/auth** — Prisma client generated to the non-default `src/generated/prisma`, JWT cookies, middleware-guarded routes, server actions.
- **Anonymous → authenticated flow** via sessionStorage hand-off.

**Conventions** — `@/` alias, colocated `__tests__/`, jsdom + RTL, shadcn-style `ui/` primitives.

I deliberately left out generic advice, exhaustive file listings, and anything trivially discoverable. There was no existing CLAUDE.md to revise.
